title: festuca pratensis var. fallax
intro: Festuca pratensis var. fallax, also known as false meadow-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of false meadow-grass include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, false meadow-grass can be used in teas, infusions, or as a dried powder, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
